 {"id":"bd-1k7c5","title":"Perf: Profile + Optimize Layout/Render Hotspots","description":"Background
- Layout and routing can be CPU-heavy on large graphs.
- Must follow extreme optimization loop: baseline → profile → single lever → verify.

Scope
- Baseline runtime with hyperfine for large diagrams.
- Profile hotspots (cargo flamegraph or perf).
- Opportunity matrix; implement only score ≥ 2.0 changes.
- Verify deterministic output via golden checksums.
- Track memory allocations and cache hit rates.

Considerations
- One lever per change; no behavior drift.
- Capture before/after metrics in comments or logs.

Success Criteria
- Measured speedup without output changes.
- Golden checksum verification passes.","notes":"Progress 2026-02-05: fixed clippy in mermaid_render (if-let chains for selection highlight + tests; removed unused imports). Fixed mermaid_showcase compile issues (import mermaid module; use std::time::Instant via fully qualified paths). Quality gates: cargo clippy/check/fmt --check all pass. Latest perf snapshot (from prior run): hyperfine mean ~171.7ms for perf_large_graph_layout; alloc stats: allocs 1,317,200, bytes_allocated 664,858,000, reallocs 424,000, bytes_reallocated 123,062,400; perf report hotspots: evaluate_layout ~19.9%, Vec<usize>::extend_with ~16%, malloc_consolidate ~10%.
Progress 2026-02-05: set Mermaid render default to Braille (MermaidConfig default + mermaid_showcase/mermaid_mega_showcase defaults + updated tests). Fixed clippy collapsible-if in mermaid_mega_showcase tests and adjusted mermaid_layout free-spot scan (array candidates) to satisfy clippy/compile. Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings all pass.

Progress 2026-02-06: mermaid_layout micro-alloc cleanup (no behavior change):
- Avoid building node_id_map HashMap when constraints are empty.
- Avoid allocating a temporary Vec in compute_bounding_box.

perf_large_graph_layout (release, 200 runs):
- allocs: 1,018,600 -> 1,018,200
- bytes_allocated: 215,574,000 -> 212,530,800
- reallocs: 424,000 (unchanged)
- checksum: 0x00000000004dff30 (unchanged)

Quality gates: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p ftui-extras --features diagram.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkRiver","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:54.455890177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T21:15:04.436569401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T21:15:04.436547831Z","close_reason":"Completed: perf harness passes; golden checksum unchanged; prior optimization work landed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:48:23.301689889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:48:15.454401150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","depends_on_id":"bd-2hxl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:48:19.163522910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","depends_on_id":"bd-u4fgt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:48:28.240854814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":803,"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: Implemented pre-allocated scratch buffer optimization for crossing-count hot paths in both diagram_layout.rs and mermaid_layout.rs. Results: layout/medium -35%, layout/large -21%, minimize_crossings -33%. All 513 tests pass, determinism verified, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T23:57:49Z"},{"id":804,"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: Core optimization complete (commit 149fd945). Remaining targets for future work: A* route_all_edges per-edge grid alloc, compact_positions O(V^2) overlap check, place_labels brute-force collision. Also: no benchmark exists for mermaid_layout production path (only diagram_layout is benchmarked).","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:00:54Z"},{"id":805,"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: Added RoutingScratch for A* routing with sparse reset (commit b242dd93). Also created mermaid_layout_bench.rs - Criterion benchmarks for the production mermaid layout path. Baselines: mermaid_layout/large/100 ~116us, astar_routing/large ~714us.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:17:50Z"},{"id":835,"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: Added LabelGrid spatial index for place_labels (commit 0244da98). Collision detection now O(bucket_size) vs O(occupied_count). Also simplified compact_positions with is_horizontal bool. 1930+1416 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:04:24Z"},{"id":995,"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Taking a pass to unblock bd-hudcn: running perf harness (perf_large_graph_layout) to verify golden checksum still holds and capture alloc stats; if clean, I’ll close bd-1k7c5.","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:14:14Z"},{"id":996,"issue_id":"bd-1k7c5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ran perf harness (release, --ignored): perf_large_graph_layout OK.  {"id":"bd-1l0g9","title":"UX: 80x24 legibility + keyboard accessibility audit","description":"Goal

Deliverables

 {"id":"bd-1lzl8","title":"Samples: tagging taxonomy + naming conventions","description":"Goal
- Define a consistent tagging taxonomy and naming conventions for Mermaid samples.

Deliverables
- Tag categories (type, complexity, stress, unicode, labels, nested, dense).
- Naming convention for sample IDs and display names.
- Validation rules enforced by loader or tests.

Acceptance
- Samples follow consistent naming/tagging; filter/search behaves predictably.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:34:48.123812192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:43:14.347080515Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:43:14.347062992Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","samples","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lzl8","depends_on_id":"bd-2sp01","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:34:49.980712071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":794,"issue_id":"bd-1lzl8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will define the tagging taxonomy and naming conventions for Mermaid samples based on existing sample metadata schema.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:38:04Z"},{"id":796,"issue_id":"bd-1lzl8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear: Implementation complete. Added:
1. KNOWN_TAGS constant - 33 tags organized into 6 semantic categories (Structure, Content, Relationships, Density, Rendering, Fallback) with doc comments explaining the taxonomy
2. KNOWN_EDGE_CASE_TAGS constant - 13 edge-case tags for regression coverage tracking
3. 9 new validation tests:
   - all_sample_tags_are_known: validates tags field against KNOWN_TAGS
   - all_sample_edge_cases_are_known: validates edge_cases against KNOWN_EDGE_CASE_TAGS
   - known_tags_are_sorted_within_categories: no duplicates in any tag list
   - sample_ids_are_kebab_case: IDs must be lowercase-dash format
   - sample_ids_start_with_family_prefix: IDs start with family name (skip unsupported)
   - sample_names_are_title_case: names must be Title Case
   - each_sample_has_at_least_one_tag: no empty tags
   - each_sample_has_at_least_one_feature: no empty features
   - known_tags_all_exercised: at most 5 unused tags allowed

All 165 mermaid_showcase tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:43:09Z"}]}
@@ -580,6 +581,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2my1.6","title":"Reactive Bindings E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of reactive data binding system.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. One-Way Binding (bind_oneway_e2e.rs)
- Observable to widget property
- Transform function application
- Update propagation timing
- Multiple bindings to same source

### 2. Two-Way Binding (bind_twoway_e2e.rs)
- Widget to Observable sync
- Observable to widget sync
- Cycle prevention
- Conflicting update handling

### 3. Computed Values (bind_computed_e2e.rs)
- Single dependency computed
- Multi-dependency computed
- Lazy evaluation
- Memoization correctness

### 4. Batch Updates (bind_batch_e2e.rs)
- Multiple updates coalesced
- Nested batch scopes
- Intermediate state hidden
- Flush timing

### 5. Lifecycle (bind_lifecycle_e2e.rs)
- Binding cleanup on unmount
- No memory leaks
- Weak reference behavior
- Debug tooling accuracy

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Binding create/destroy
- DEBUG: Update propagation path
- TRACE: Computed recalculations
- Metric: Binding count over time

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/reactive_bindings/

## Memory Tests
- Long-running binding churn
- Leak detection via valgrind
- Binding graph visualization

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Reactive Bindings E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-2nkmi","title":"Mermaid Showcase Super Screen (over-the-top interactive demo)","description":"Purpose
- Build a new, over-the-top interactive demo screen that makes the Mermaid pipeline visible and impressive: parse -> IR -> layout -> render, with live controls and performance instrumentation.
- Demonstrate fast, deterministic recomputation of "optimal" layouts across many complex samples.

User Value / Why
- Turns the existing mermaid capabilities in ftui-extras into a flagship, testable, keyboard-first demo.
- Gives clear evidence of correctness, determinism, and performance budgets.

Scope
- New screen in ftui-demo-showcase with rich UI (header, sample list, viewport, controls, metrics, status log).
- Large curated sample library spanning multiple Mermaid diagram types and edge cases.
- Interactive controls for layout/fidelity/viewport (including viewport size overrides) and a metrics panel showing timing + quality.

Non-Goals
- No full Mermaid editor; samples are read-only.
- No backward compat shims; update core code directly if needed.

Acceptance Criteria
- Screen renders complex Mermaid examples deterministically for fixed inputs.
- Layout/fidelity/viewport changes visibly update output and metrics.
- Metrics panel shows parse/layout/render times and layout quality fields.
- Works at 80x24 (minimal), 120x40 (default), 200x60 (max showcase).
- Unit tests, snapshots, and e2e harness runs validate behavior with detailed logs.

Testing Requirements (must be included by child tasks)
- Unit tests: state transitions, keybindings, rendering triggers, error handling.
- Snapshot tests: 3+ sizes x 3+ diagram types, with metrics visibility + viewport size overrides.
- E2E scripts: deterministic seed, exit-after, JSONL log validation, clear failure messages.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:20:58.099456610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:07:13.142443975Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T03:07:13.142416163Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","screen"],"comments":[{"id":831,"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: All 11 sub-beads closed. The Mermaid Showcase Super Screen (mega showcase) is fully implemented with: interactive controls, keybindings, render pipeline integration, performance/telemetry overlay with color-coded thresholds, layout optimization showcase, comparison mode, UI layout+theming, comprehensive unit tests (236), E2E tests (31), and JSONL telemetry emission.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:07:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.1","title":"Spec the Mermaid showcase screen UX + interaction model","description":"What (spec already drafted in docs/spec/mermaid-showcase.md)
- This bead captures the UX + interaction model and acts as source of truth for implementation.

Key UX decisions (summary, keep in-sync with spec file)
- Screen regions: header (title, sample index, layout, fidelity, status), sample list, render viewport, control panel, metrics panel, footer hints.
- Layout rules by size:
  - 80x24: collapsed sample selector, compact metrics line, control overlay toggle.
  - 120x40: 3-column layout (sample list | viewport | controls+metrics).
  - 200x60: expanded layout with optional status log panel.
- Keybindings (required set):
  - Sample nav: Up/Down or j/k, Home/End, Enter (re-render)
  - Zoom: +/-, 0 reset, f fit
  - Viewport size override: [ / ] adjust width, { / } adjust height (or equivalent explicit mapping)
  - Layout: l cycle mode (Auto/Dense/Spacious), r force re-layout
  - Panels: m toggle metrics, c toggle controls
  - Fidelity: t cycle tier, g glyph mode, s styles, w wrap mode
  - Help: ? overlay, Esc close overlay
- State model: selected_index, layout_mode, fidelity, glyph_mode, wrap_mode, styles_enabled, metrics_visible, controls_visible,
  viewport_zoom/pan, viewport_size_override (w,h), last_render report, last_error.
- Metrics fields: parse_ms, layout_ms, render_ms, layout_iterations, objective_score, constraint_violations, fallback_tier, fallback_reason.

Acceptance
- Spec is self-contained and includes the above details; no external plan needed.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:20:58.343477747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:06:46.236718623Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T14:26:34.288305432Z","close_reason":"Spec delivered in docs/spec/mermaid-showcase.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2nkmi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:20:58.343477747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":718,"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting UX/interaction spec for Mermaid showcase screen. MCP Agent Mail resources are not available in this environment, so I will coordinate via Beads comments. Next: draft spec doc under docs/spec/ and outline layout, keybindings, metrics, and size behavior.","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:24:39Z"},{"id":719,"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Drafted spec in docs/spec/mermaid-showcase.md with goals, layout by size (80x24/120x40/200x60), keybindings, metrics list, and state model.","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:26:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.10","title":"E2E harness coverage for Mermaid showcase","description":"What
- Add e2e harness coverage for the Mermaid screen.

E2E requirements
- Run screen with fixed seed and exit-after.
- Capture structured logs and validate JSONL schema + output hash.

@@ -1241,7 +1243,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-9ta1z","title":"Diagram Type: Mindmap","description":"Background
- Mindmaps are visually rich and benefit from adaptive layout at large sizes.

Scope

Considerations

- Deterministic branch ordering.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for tree parsing + layout ordering.
- Snapshot tests for 3-level mindmaps.
- PTY E2E logs: diagram_type=mindmap, size, tier, warnings.

Success Criteria

 {"id":"bd-9xbn","title":"Layout Constraint Debugger","description":"# Layout Constraint Debugger

## Background & Motivation

Layout bugs are notoriously hard to debug:
- Constraints are implicit in code
- Computed values aren't visible
- Overflows and underflows silent

**The solution:**
- Visual debugger for layout constraints
- Shows requested vs received sizes
- Highlights overflow/underflow in red
- Tree view of layout hierarchy

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-3ky.4 (highlight theme system)
- Requires bd-yzu (Flex layout with constraint solving)
- Complements bd-17nn (Widget Introspection Debug Overlay)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct LayoutDebugger {
    enabled: bool,
    records: Vec<LayoutRecord>,
}

pub struct LayoutRecord {
    widget_name: String,
    area_requested: Rect,
    area_received: Rect,
    constraints: LayoutConstraints,
    children: Vec<LayoutRecord>,
}

pub struct LayoutConstraints {
    min_width: u16,
    max_width: u16,
    min_height: u16,
    max_height: u16,
}

impl LayoutDebugger {
    pub fn record(&mut self, name: &str, requested: Rect, received: Rect, constraints: LayoutConstraints) { ... }
    pub fn render_debug(&self, buf: &mut Buffer) { ... }
    pub fn export_dot(&self) -> String { ... }  // Graphviz export
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Constraint violation (WARN level)
tracing::warn!(
    widget = name,
    requested = ?requested,
    received = ?received,
    "Layout constraint violation"
);

// Layout computed (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(
    widget = name,
    constraints = ?constraints,
    result = ?area,
    "Layout computed"
);
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Enable layout debugger and run complex layout
./ftui-harness --layout-debugger --layout complex_grid

# Export constraint graph
./ftui-harness --layout-debugger --export-dot layout.dot

# Verify no constraint violations
./scripts/verify-layout.sh --assert-no-violations

# Visual regression test
./ftui-harness --layout-debugger --screenshot layout_debug.png
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Constraint recording works for all widgets
- [ ] Overflow highlighted in red
- [ ] Underflow highlighted in yellow
- [ ] Computed values visible alongside constraints
- [ ] Tree view of layout hierarchy
- [ ] Graphviz export for documentation
- [ ] Zero overhead when disabled
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:16.766323253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:27:52.620510379Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:27:52.620485482Z","close_reason":"Layout constraint debugger implemented with LayoutRecord, GridLayoutRecord, LayoutDebugger, overflow/underflow detection, utilization tracking, and Graphviz DOT export","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9xbn","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9xbn","depends_on_id":"bd-yzu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-a8wk","title":"Keybinding Hints: Incremental Layout Cache + Dirty-Rect Updates","description":"## Goal
Make hint rendering effectively free when focus changes or usage updates by reusing layout and updating only dirty regions.

## Core Idea (Extreme Optimization)
- Cache layout measurement for each hint group (keys + labels).\n- Maintain a dirty-rect list for hint panel; only recompute sections that change.\n- Use stable ordering and fixed cell widths to avoid reflow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No full re-layout on focus change unless hint set changes.\n- Zero allocations on steady-state updates.\n- O(k) updates for k changed hints, not O(n).

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_cache_hit_same_hints: layout reused on identical hint set.\n- unit_dirty_rect_only_changes: only modified hints are repainted.\n- perf_micro_hint_update: p50/p95 update cost logged under 1000 updates/sec.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Focus-change storm with logs: layout hits/misses, dirty-rect sizes, frame time.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:13:03.313936637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:19:36.704854906Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:19:36.704835620Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-agng","title":"Implement recovery mechanism for budget upgrade","description":"# Implement recovery mechanism for budget upgrade

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). When load decreases, the system should recover to higher fidelity. This must be gradual and stable.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Budget recovery tracker
pub struct BudgetRecovery {
    /// Current degradation level
    current_level: DegradationLevel,
    /// Frames at current level under budget
    stable_frames: u32,
    /// Required stable frames before upgrade
    threshold: u32,
    /// Frames since last degradation
    frames_since_degrade: u32,
    /// Minimum frames before allowing upgrade after degradation
    min_recovery_delay: u32,
}

impl BudgetRecovery {
    pub fn new(threshold: u32) -> Self {
        Self {
            current_level: DegradationLevel::Full,
            stable_frames: 0,
            threshold,
            frames_since_degrade: u32::MAX / 2,  // Start as if long stable
            min_recovery_delay: 10,
        }
    }
    
    /// Record a frame that finished under budget
    pub fn record_under_budget(&mut self) {
        self.stable_frames = self.stable_frames.saturating_add(1);
        self.frames_since_degrade = self.frames_since_degrade.saturating_add(1);
    }
    
    /// Record a frame that exceeded budget
    pub fn record_over_budget(&mut self) {
        self.stable_frames = 0;
    }
    
    /// Check if we should upgrade to higher fidelity
    pub fn should_upgrade(&self) -> bool {
        self.current_level != DegradationLevel::Full
            && self.stable_frames >= self.threshold
            && self.frames_since_degrade >= self.min_recovery_delay
    }
    
    /// Perform upgrade
    pub fn upgrade(&mut self) {
        if self.current_level != DegradationLevel::Full {
            let old = self.current_level;
            self.current_level = self.current_level.upgrade();
            self.stable_frames = 0;  // Reset stability counter
            
            tracing::info!(
                from = ?old,
                to = ?self.current_level,
                "Upgrading render fidelity after stable performance"
            );
        }
    }
    
    /// Record degradation (resets recovery progress)
    pub fn on_degrade(&mut self, new_level: DegradationLevel) {
        self.current_level = new_level;
        self.stable_frames = 0;
        self.frames_since_degrade = 0;
    }
}
```

## Why Recovery Delay?
- Prevents ping-pong between levels
- Allows system to stabilize after load spike
- Users see gradual improvement, not flickering

## Test Requirements
- [ ] stable_frames increments on under_budget
- [ ] stable_frames resets on over_budget
- [ ] should_upgrade requires threshold AND delay
- [ ] upgrade() advances level and logs
- [ ] on_degrade() resets all counters

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BudgetRecovery struct implemented
- [ ] Gradual upgrade (one level at a time)
- [ ] Stability threshold enforced
- [ ] Recovery delay prevents oscillation
- [ ] Logging on upgrade events","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:21.960433851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:18:41.208616362Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:18:41.208597797Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-1iqn in budget.rs - should_upgrade(), upgrade(), cooldown tracking in RenderBudget struct","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-agng","depends_on_id":"bd-3tfu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-atwq","title":"Text Effects: Chromatic Aberration / RGB Split","description":"## Background
Chromatic aberration (RGB channel separation) is a popular effect in modern UI design, creating a '3D glasses' or glitch aesthetic. High visual impact for low implementation effort.

## Implementation

Add TextEffect variant:
```rust
TextEffect::ChromaticAberration {
    offset: u8,           // Cell offset 1-5 (typically 1-2)
    direction: Direction, // Which way red/blue shift
    animated: bool,       // Oscillate offset over time
    speed: f64,           // Oscillations per second if animated
}
```

### Rendering Approach (Terminal-Friendly):

Since terminals use character cells (not pixels), we simulate chromatic aberration by color-shifting based on position:

```rust
fn chromatic_color(&self, base_color: PackedRgba, idx: usize, total: usize) -> PackedRgba {
    let offset = self.offset as f64;
    let center = total as f64 / 2.0;
    let distance_from_center = (idx as f64 - center) / center;  // -1 to 1
    
    // Left side: boost red, reduce blue
    // Right side: boost blue, reduce red
    let red_boost = (-distance_from_center * offset * 30.0).clamp(-50.0, 50.0);
    let blue_boost = (distance_from_center * offset * 30.0).clamp(-50.0, 50.0);
    
    PackedRgba::rgb(
        (base_color.r() as i16 + red_boost as i16).clamp(0, 255) as u8,
        base_color.g(),
        (base_color.b() as i16 + blue_boost as i16).clamp(0, 255) as u8,
    )
}
```

### Direction Options:
- Horizontal (default): Red left, Blue right
- Vertical: Red top, Blue bottom

### Animation:
When animated=true, offset oscillates: actual_offset = offset * sin(time * speed * 2π)

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_chromatic_offset_0_normal: No color shift at offset 0
- test_chromatic_left_chars_redder: Left side has more red
- test_chromatic_right_chars_bluer: Right side has more blue
- test_chromatic_center_unchanged: Center char minimal shift
- test_chromatic_animated_varies: Different time = different shift
- test_chromatic_offset_clamped: Colors stay in 0-255 range
- test_chromatic_direction_vertical: Top/bottom shift instead

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot 'ABERRATION' with offset=2
- Snapshot same text at t=0 vs t=0.5 with animation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Chromatic Aberration / RGB Split
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
